content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes three integers as input and determines the largest among them. Print the largest number.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space-separated integers denoting the three numbers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line in the format "Largest: X" where X is the greatest of the three given integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three numbers are 1, 2 and 3; the greatest is 3.
Among 10, 5 and 8 the maximum value is 10.
All three numbers are equal, so the largest is 7.

solution.approach: We simply need the maximum of three numbers for each test case. For each test case we read the three integers, keep a variable mx initialized to the first number and compare it with the second and third numbers, updating mx whenever a larger value is found. Finally we output "Largest: mx". This runs in O(1) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int a=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int b=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int c=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int mx=a;if(b>mx)mx=b;if(c>mx)mx=c;sb.append("Largest: ").append(mx);if(t<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
